comment.body: Although I don’t full understand the mechanics, I am starting to subscribe to the increasing liquidity narrative as the reason for asset prices increasing.  China certainly injecting liquidity and M2 is up 4.5% this year, plus the treasury buying down long end and issuing short end.  The feel is so much like post COVID in terms of price appreciation across all asset classes.
comment.raw_body: Although I don’t full understand the mechanics, I am starting to subscribe to the increasing liquidity narrative as the reason for asset prices increasing. comment.body: It masks underlying liquidity issue. Thats why they're hell-bent on trying to get a cut. Once you get the rate cut, you don't need to aggressively supply liqudiity because it gets unlocked from money market funds (lower fed rate means MMF's get lower yield so some of that money rotates into markets)
comment.raw_body: It masks underlying liquidity issue. Thats why they're hell-bent on trying to get a cut.
